key_points: Heredity explains how traits are passed from parents to offspring through genes
Genes are located on chromosomes and control specific characteristics
Mendel conducted experiments on pea plants to understand inheritance patterns
Alleles are different forms of the same gene
Traits may be dominant or recessive depending on allele interaction
Genotype refers to genetic makeup while phenotype refers to observable traits
Variation among individuals arises due to genetic recombination during reproduction
examples: In pea plants, tallness (T) is dominant over dwarfness (t)
Cross between TT and tt produces Tt offspring which are tall
In F2 generation, plants show 3 tall : 1 dwarf ratio
AB blood group shows codominance where both alleles express equally
Pink flowers in snapdragon plants show incomplete dominance
formulas: Current = I = Q/t (used in genetics analogy for gene transfer rate)
Genotype → Genetic constitution of an organism
exam_tips: Practice drawing Punnett squares for monohybrid and dihybrid crosses
Understand differences between genotype and phenotype clearly
Remember examples of incomplete dominance and codominance
Understand how variations help in evolution and survival
